meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Scale Development
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'basic app' plateau. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional, high-converting apps faster.
content.answer_block: Stop building from scratch. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you unify authentication, database schemas, and billing logic into a repeatable architecture. Use Antigravity to scaffold your codebase and Claude Code to automate complex feature implementation, cutting your development cycle by 70% while maintaining enterprise-grade code quality.
content.intro:
You’re staring at yet another boilerplate setup, watching your billable hours evaporate while you manually wire up Auth0 and Stripe for the tenth time. The 'basic app' trap is a silent career killer for freelancers; you’re stuck in a loop of low-value grunt work instead of shipping high-impact products that command premium fees.

But the tide is turning. By shifting from manual coding to an architecture-first approach using a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you transform from a laborer into a velocity-driven architect. This isn't just about faster typing; it's about deploying production-ready systems that allow you to scale your freelance business without burning out.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Structure
steps.description: Initialize your project using a production-ready starter kit to ensure your foundation is scalable and secure from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular SaaS architecture using Next.js 14, Supabase for backend, and Stripe for payments. Ensure the directory structure follows domain-driven design principles.

steps.title: Implementing Authentication Flows
steps.description: Standardize your auth to handle multi-tenant users, preventing the common 'basic app' mistake of hardcoded user logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure multi-tenant authentication with Supabase Auth, including protected routes and user role-based access control (RBAC).
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement middleware for protected routes and enforce session persistence'

steps.title: Automating Database Schema Migrations
steps.description: Use AI to define your database relationships, ensuring data integrity across billing, user profiles, and app-specific features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a PostgreSQL schema for a SaaS platform with tables for subscriptions, organizations, and feature flags. Include cascading deletes and audit logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate migration files for users, subscriptions, and team tables'

steps.title: Integrating Stripe Webhooks
steps.description: Seamlessly sync your database with Stripe events to handle subscription lifecycles automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler that updates user subscription status in Supabase based on invoice.payment_succeeded and customer.subscription.deleted events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add stripe webhook endpoint and logic to update subscription status'

steps.title: Optimizing for Production Velocity
steps.description: Refine your build pipeline to ensure lightning-fast CI/CD, allowing you to ship features to clients daily.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel, including linting and type-checking steps.

pro_tips: Always wrap your external API calls in a dedicated service layer to make swapping providers painless.
Use Zod schemas for all client-server communication to prevent runtime type errors before they reach production.
Implement 'feature flags' early to allow your clients to toggle beta features without pushing new code.
Standardize your error handling globally; unhandled promise rejections are the primary cause of freelancer support tickets.
Pre-build a library of UI components that match your specific design system to avoid re-styling from scratch.
content.next_steps: After completing your first build, document your custom configuration as a personal boilerplate. This turns your project into a reusable asset for future freelance clients, exponentially increasing your profit margin on every subsequent project.
content.conversion_block: Stop settling for basic. Elevate your development workflow, command higher rates, and build products that actually scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
